sages.name: Men of the Great Assembly
sages.era: Early Second Temple · c. 5th–3rd cent. BCE
sages.bio: The assembly of 120 sages and prophets who led Israel at the dawn of the Second Temple era and shaped prayer and Torah transmission.

sages.name: Shimon the Righteous
sages.bio: High Priest and one of the last members of the Great Assembly.

sages.name: Antigonus of Socho
sages.bio: Disciple of Shimon the Righteous; the earliest sage of the chain to bear a Greek name.

sages.name: Yose ben Yoezer
sages.bio: Of Zeredah; Nasi in the first of the Zugot — the five 'pairs' who carried the tradition.

sages.name: Yose ben Yochanan
sages.bio: Of Jerusalem; Av Beit Din alongside Yose ben Yoezer in the first of the Zugot.

sages.name: Yehoshua ben Perachiah
sages.bio: Nasi in the second of the Zugot.

sages.name: Nittai the Arbelite
sages.bio: Av Beit Din in the second of the Zugot, from Arbel in the Galilee.

sages.name: Yehudah ben Tabbai
sages.bio: Nasi in the third of the Zugot, in the turbulent Hasmonean years.

sages.name: Shimon ben Shetach
sages.bio: Restored Torah law and courts under Queen Shlomtzion; champion of rigorous testimony.

sages.name: Shemaiah
sages.bio: Nasi in the fourth of the Zugot; with Avtalyon, teacher of Hillel and Shammai.

sages.name: Avtalyon
sages.bio: Av Beit Din in the fourth of the Zugot; tradition counts him a descendant of converts.

sages.name: Hillel
sages.era: Last of the Zugot · late 1st cent. BCE
sages.bio: Babylonian-born Nasi famed for humility and patience; founder of Beit Hillel.

sages.name: Shammai
sages.era: Last of the Zugot · late 1st cent. BCE
sages.bio: Av Beit Din opposite Hillel; founder of Beit Shammai, known for exacting rigor.

sages.name: Rabban Gamliel the Elder
sages.bio: Grandson of Hillel and Nasi of the Sanhedrin in the late Second Temple period.

sages.name: Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel
sages.bio: Nasi of the Sanhedrin in Hillel's line — the name spans the father martyred around the Temple's fall and the grandson who led at Usha.

sages.name: Rabbi Yehudah HaNasi
sages.bio: 'Rabbi' — redactor of the Mishnah and Nasi, revered simply as 'our holy teacher.'

sages.name: Rabban Gamliel ben Rabbi Yehudah HaNasi
sages.hebrew: רַבָּן גַּמְלִיאֵל בְּנוֹ שֶׁל רַבִּי יְהוּדָה הַנָּשִׂיא
sages.bio: Son of Rabbi Yehudah HaNasi who succeeded him as Nasi.

sages.name: Rabban Yochanan ben Zakkai
sages.bio: Youngest disciple of Hillel; rescued Torah leadership at Yavneh after the Temple's destruction.

sages.name: Rabbi Eliezer ben Hyrcanus
sages.bio: 'A plastered cistern that loses not a drop' — master of received tradition among Rabban Yochanan ben Zakkai's five disciples.

sages.name: Rabbi Yehoshua ben Chananiah
sages.bio: 'Happy is she who bore him' — Levite singer in the Temple and leading sage of Yavneh.

sages.name: Rabbi Yose HaKohen
sages.bio: 'The pious one' (chasid) among Rabban Yochanan ben Zakkai's five disciples.

sages.name: Rabbi Shimon ben Netanel
sages.bio: 'Fearer of sin' among Rabban Yochanan ben Zakkai's five disciples.

sages.name: Rabbi Elazar ben Arach
sages.bio: 'An ever-flowing spring' — rated by his master as outweighing all the sages of Israel.

sages.name: Rabbi Tarfon
sages.bio: Priest of Yavneh and colleague-rival of Rabbi Akiva, famed for generosity.

sages.name: Akavya ben Mahalalel
sages.bio: Early sage who would not retract his rulings even to become Av Beit Din.

sages.name: Rabbi Chanina, Deputy of the Priests
sages.bio: Deputy High Priest who witnessed the Temple's final years and taught from its service.

sages.name: Rabbi Chananiah ben Teradion
sages.bio: One of the Ten Martyrs, burned wrapped in a Torah scroll; father of Beruriah.

sages.name: Rabbi Shimon bar Yochai
sages.bio: Disciple of Rabbi Akiva who hid thirteen years in a cave; the Zohar speaks in his name.

sages.name: Rabbi Chananiah ben Chakhinai
sages.bio: Disciple of Rabbi Akiva who studied away from home for twelve years.

sages.name: Rabbi Nechunia ben HaKanah
sages.bio: Yavneh-era sage whose daily prayers on entering and leaving the study hall are preserved in the Talmud.

sages.name: Rabbi Chalafta of Kfar Chananiah
sages.bio: Galilean sage of the generation after the Bar Kokhba revolt.

sages.name: Rabbi Elazar of Bartota
sages.bio: So lavish in charity that the collectors would hide from him.

sages.name: Rabbi Yaakov
sages.bio: Rabbi Yaakov ben Korshai, a teacher of Rabbi Yehudah HaNasi.

sages.name: Rabbi Dostai ben Yannai
sages.bio: Disciple who transmitted teachings in the name of Rabbi Meir.

sages.name: Rabbi Meir
sages.bio: Master of law and parable — anonymous mishnayot follow his teaching; husband of Beruriah.

sages.name: Rabbi Chanina ben Dosa
sages.bio: Wonder-working chasid of the Galilee who lived on a measure of carobs from one Shabbat to the next.

sages.name: Rabbi Dosa ben Harkinas
sages.bio: Long-lived sage whose years spanned the Temple's destruction and the academy at Yavneh.

sages.name: Rabbi Elazar of Modiin
sages.bio: Master of aggadah at Yavneh; tradition places his death in besieged Beitar.

sages.name: Rabbi Yishmael
sages.bio: Rabbi Yishmael ben Elisha, builder of a school of midrash — 'the Torah speaks in human language.'

sages.name: Rabbi Akiva
sages.bio: The shepherd who began learning at forty and became the foundation of the Mishnah; martyred by Rome.

sages.name: Rabbi Elazar ben Azariah
sages.bio: Priest of ten generations' lineage, appointed Nasi at Yavneh while still a young man.

sages.name: Rabbi Eliezer Chisma
sages.bio: Mathematician-sage who mastered astronomy and geometry yet called them 'side dishes to wisdom.'

sages.name: Ben Zoma
sages.bio: Shimon ben Zoma, master expositor who entered the Pardes; died before ordination.

sages.name: Ben Azzai
sages.bio: Shimon ben Azzai, wholly devoted to Torah study; of him it is said he 'gazed and died' in the Pardes.

sages.name: Rabbi Levitas of Yavneh
sages.bio: Yavneh sage remembered for a single teaching of radical humility.

sages.name: Rabbi Yochanan ben Beroka
sages.bio: Disciple of Rabbi Yehoshua at Yavneh.

sages.name: Rabbi Yishmael ben Rabbi Yochanan ben Beroka
sages.hebrew: רַבִּי יִשְׁמָעֵאל בְּנוֹ שֶׁל רַבִּי יוֹחָנָן בֶּן בְּרוֹקָא
sages.bio: Son of Rabbi Yochanan ben Beroka, of the Usha generation.

sages.name: Rabbi Tzadok
sages.bio: Priest who, tradition says, fasted forty years to avert Jerusalem's destruction.

sages.name: Rabbi Yose
sages.bio: Rabbi Yose ben Chalafta of Tzippori — the law follows him; credited with Seder Olam.

sages.name: Rabbi Yishmael ben Rabbi Yose
sages.bio: Son of Rabbi Yose ben Chalafta, a judge famed for integrity.

sages.name: Rabbi Yonatan
sages.bio: Of the school of Rabbi Yishmael.

sages.name: Rabbi Eliezer ben Yaakov
sages.bio: His teaching is called 'small in measure and clean' (kav v'naki) — sparse, and always the law.

sages.name: Rabbi Yochanan HaSandlar
sages.bio: The sandal-maker of Alexandria, a disciple of Rabbi Akiva.

sages.name: Rabbi Elazar ben Shammua
sages.bio: Priest among the last disciples of Rabbi Akiva; a teacher of Rabbi Yehudah HaNasi.

sages.name: Rabbi Yehudah
sages.bio: Rabbi Yehudah bar Ilai — 'first speaker on every occasion' in the academy at Usha.

sages.name: Rabbi Nehorai
sages.bio: 'The illuminator' — held by some to be a byname of Rabbi Meir or Rabbi Nechemiah.

sages.name: Rabbi Yannai
sages.bio: His one teaching here confronts the mystery of the righteous who suffer.

sages.name: Rabbi Matya ben Charash
sages.bio: Founded the first Torah academy in Rome.

sages.name: Rabbi Shimon ben Elazar
sages.bio: Disciple of Rabbi Meir, of the Mishnah's final generation.

sages.name: Shmuel HaKatan
sages.bio: 'The small,' for his humility; composed the Yavneh blessing against informers.

sages.name: Elisha ben Avuyah
sages.bio: The sage who entered the Pardes and became 'Acher,' the Other; Rabbi Meir never stopped learning from him.

sages.name: Rabbi Yose ben Yehudah
sages.bio: Of Kfar HaBavli; a colleague of Rabbi Yehudah HaNasi.

sages.name: Rabbi Elazar HaKappar
sages.bio: Of the Mishnah's last generation; father of Bar Kappara.

sages.name: Yehudah ben Tema
sages.bio: Author of the ladder of the ages of man — 'at five, Scripture; at ten, Mishnah…'

sages.name: Ben Bag Bag
sages.bio: Yochanan ben Bag Bag, counted by tradition among the converts who learned under Hillel.

sages.name: Ben Hei Hei
sages.bio: Like Ben Bag Bag, tradition places him among the converts taught by Hillel.

sages.name: Rabbi Yehoshua ben Levi
sages.bio: Amora of Lod and hero of aggadah — said to have entered the Garden of Eden alive.

sages.name: Rabbi Shimon ben Menasya
sages.bio: Of the 'holy congregation' that divided each day between Torah, prayer, and labor.

sages.name: Rabbi Yose ben Kisma
sages.bio: Refused all the riches in the world rather than live outside a place of Torah.

chain.title: From Sinai
chain.note: Moses received the Torah at Sinai and transmitted it (Avot 1:1)

chain.title: The Zugot — Five Pairs
chain.note: In each pair: the Nasi (president) and the Av Beit Din (head of court)

chain.title: The House of Hillel — the Dynasty of the Nesi'im
After the Destruction — Yavneh
chain.note: Rabban Yochanan ben Zakkai and his five disciples (Avot 2:8)
